I say to you, you shall not come out from there until you shall have paid the last lepton (small piece of money).
And some were present at the same time telling Him about the Galileans whose blood Pilate had mingled with their sacrifices.
And answering, He said to them, Do you suppose that these Galileans were sinners beyond all the Galileans, because they have suffered such things?
No, I say to you; but if you do not repent, you will all likewise perish.
Or those eighteen upon whom the tower in Siloam fell and killed them: do you suppose these were debtors beyond all the men residing in Jerusalem?
No, I say to you; but if you do not repent, you will all likewise perish.
And He spoke this parable: A certain man had planted a fig tree in his vineyard; and he came seeking fruit on (in) it and did not find any.

And he said to the vinedresser, Behold, from these three years I come seeking fruit on (in) this fig tree and do not find any. Therefore cut it down, so that- Why should it render the ground inoperative (useless, of no effect)?
And answering, he says to him, Lord, let it alone this year until when I shall dig around it and throw in manure,
And if it should make fruit unto the [time] about to be; however it not, you will cut it out.
And He was teaching in one of the synagogues on the Sabbath.
And behold, a woman having a spirit of infirmity (weakness, frailty, illness, sickness) eighteen years. And she was bent over and not able to lift herself up unto a whole-end (entire way).
And Jesus having seen her called to her and said to her, Woman, you have been freed (released, divorced) from your infirmity.
And He laid his hands upon her, and instantly she was made straight, and glorified God.

And the ruler of the synagogue, indignant because Jesus healed on the Sabbath, answering, said to the crowd, There are six days in which it is necessary for people to work; in these therefore coming be healed, and not on the day of the Sabbath.
The Lord therefore answered him, and said, Hypocrites! Does not each one of you untie his ox or donkey from the stall and having led it away, give it to drink?
And this woman, being a daughter of Abraham, whom Satan has bound, behold, eighteen years, ought she not be loosed from this bond on the day of the Sabbath?
And on His saying these things, all those opposed to Him were ashamed; and all the crowd was rejoicing at (upon) all the glorious things that were being done by Him.
And He said, To what is like the kingdom of God? And to what will I liken it?
It is like to a grain of mustard, which a man having taken threw into his garden; and it grew and came into a tree and the birds of the heaven roosted (nested, tented, encamped) in its branches.
And He said again, To what will I liken the kingdom of God? English gallons) of meal (flour) until the whole was leavened.
It is like leaven, which a woman having taken hid into three measures (nearly 3 English gallons) of meal (flour) until the whole was leavened.
And He went through town by town and village by village, teaching, and making journey unto Jerusalem.
And one said to Him, Lord, are those being saved few? And He said to them,
Contend (strive, struggle, latin-contendite) to enter in through the narrow door (latin-portam-gate like portal); because many, I say to you, will seek to enter in and will not be able (strong to).

From that [time that] the master of the house shall have risen up and shall have shut the door and you should begin to stand outside and knock at the door, saying, Lord, open to us; and He answering will say to you, I do not know from where you are.
Then you will begin to say, We ate and drank in your sight and You taught in our streets.
And He will say, I tell you, I do not know from where you are; depart away from Me, all workers of unrighteousness.

There will be wailing (audible bitter uncontainable grievous emotional pain out of utter hopelessness) and gnashing (grinding) of teeth, when you see Abraham, and Isaac, and Jacob, and all the prophets in the kingdom of God; you however are being cast out outside.
And they will come from east and west and from north and south and will recline in the kingdom of God.
And behold, there are last who will be first, and there are first who will be last.
In the same hour, certain Pharisees came to Him, saying to Him, Go out and proceed from here, because Herod wants to kill You.
And He said to them, Go, say to the fox that, Behold, I cast out demons and I complete healings (iaseis) today and tomorrow, and the third [day] I am finished (perfected, completed, consummated);
However it is necessary for Me, today and tomorrow and the following [day] to proceed; because it is not possible for a prophet to perish outside of Jerusalem.

Jerusalem, Jerusalem, killing the prophets, and stoning those having been sent to her, how often I have wanted to gather together those your children, that way a hen [gathers] her brood under her wings and you would not.
Behold, your house is left alone to you. And I say to you, you shall not see Me until [the time] comes when you say, Blessed is the One coming in the name of the Lord.
And it happened on His going into the house of one of the rulers of the Pharisees to eat bread on a Sabbath, and they were watching Him.
And behold, a certain man was there with dropsy (edema, swelling of soft tissues holding excess water) before Him.
And answering, Jesus spoke to the lawyers and to the Pharisees, saying, Is it permitted (lawful) on the Sabbath to heal (therapeusai) or not?
However they were silent. And having laid hold upon him, He healed (iasato) him and let him go.
And He said to them, Which of you will a son or an ox (cow) fall into a pit and not directly pull him up on the Sabbath day?
And they were not able to reply to these things.
And He spoke a parable to those having been invited, remarking how they were choosing out the first places (at a table or banquet), saying to them,
When you are invited by anyone unto weddings, do not recline in the first place, lest ever one more held in honor (valued, precious, honorable) than you might have been invited by him,
And the one having invited you and having come will say to you, Give your place to this one and then you should begin with shame to take the last place.

But when you are invited, recline in the last place, so that when the one having invited you might come, he will say to you, Friend, come up higher. Then glory will be to you in the sight of all those reclining with you;
Because everyone exalting (raising high, elevating) himself will be humbled (made low), and the one humbling himself will be exalted.

And He said also to the one having invited Him, When you make a dinner (breakfast or mid-day meal) or a supper (afternoon or evening meal), do not call your friends, nor your brothers, nor your relatives, nor rich neighbors, lest ever also they should invite you in return and be made a recompense (gift in return, reciprocate, pay-back) to you;
But when you make a feast, call the poor (beggarly, destitute), the crippled, the lame, the blind;
and you will be blessed; because they have nothing to repay (recompense) you; for it will be recompensed you in the resurrection of the righteous (just).
And one of those reclining at the table with Him having heard these things, said to Him, Blessed is he who will eat bread in the kingdom of God.
And He said to him, A certain man made a great supper and invited many.
And He sent his servant at the hour of the supper to say to those having been invited, Come, because it is already ready.
And all with one [voice] began to excuse themselves. The first said to him, I have bought a field and I have need of going out to see it; I ask you to have me excused.
And another said, I have bought five yoke of oxen, and I am going to examine (approve by testing) them; I ask you to have me excused.
And another said, I have married a wife, and because of this I am not able to come.

And having come, the servant reported these things to his lord. Then having been angry, the master of the house said to his servant, Go out quickly into the streets (wide roads) and lanes (narrow streets) of the city, and bring in here the poor, and crippled, and blind, and lame.
And the servant said, Lord, it has been done as you did command, and there is still room (place).
And the lord said to the servant, Go out into the roads and fences and compel (necessitate) them to come in, so that my house might be filled;
For I say to you, that not one of those men having been invited will taste of my supper.
And great crowds were going with Him; and having turned, He said to them,
If anyone comes to Me, and does not hate his own father, and mother, and wife, and children, and brothers, and sisters; yes and also his life (soul), he is not able to be My disciple;
Whoever does not carry his cross and come after Me is not able to be My disciple.
For which of you, wanting to build a tower, does not first having sat down, counts the cost, whether he has [enough] unto its completion?
So that having laid its foundation and not being able to finish, all looking at it should begin to mock him,
Saying, This man began to build and was not able to finish.

Or what king going to engage in war with another king does not first sit down and take counsel (determine) whether he is able with ten thousand to meet the one with twenty thousand coming against (upon) him?
And if not, his being still a far off, having sent an embassy (presbeian, delegation, eldership, latin-agente legationem- agent embassy root is deal ambassador), he asks for peace.
So therefore everyone of you who does not give up all that he possesses is not able to be My disciple.
Salt is then good, however if the salt becomes savorless (useless, without an edge), with what will it be seasoned (flavored)?
Neither unto soil, nor unto manure is it fit; they cast it outside. The one having ears to hear, let him hear.
And all the tax collectors and sinners were drawing near to hear Him;
And the Pharisees and the scribes grumbled, saying, This Man receives sinners and eats with them.
And He spoke this parable to them, saying,
What man from out of you having a hundred sheep and having lost one from out of them does not leave the ninety nine in the wilderness and goes after (upon) the one having been lost until he finds it?
And having found it, he lays it upon his shoulders, rejoicing.
And having come unto the house, he calls together his friends and neighbors, saying to them, Rejoice with me, because I have found my sheep, the one having been lost!
I say to you that thus there will be joy in heaven upon one sinner repenting, more than upon the ninety nine righteous ones who have no necessity of repentance.
Or what woman having ten drachmas, if she should lose one drachma, does not light a lamp and sweep the house and carefully (diligently) seek until she finds it?
And having found it, she calls together her friends and neighbors, saying, Rejoice with me, because I have found the drachma that I lost.
Thus, I say to you, there is joy in the sight of angels of God upon one sinner repenting.
And He said, A certain man had two sons.
And the younger of them said to the father, Father, give to me the portion of the property that is falling to me. And he divided the property between them.
And after not many days, the younger son having gathered all together went away into a distant country and there wasted his estate (property, wealth) living prodigally.
And he having spent all, there arose a severe (strong) famine throughout that country and he began to be in lack (need).
And having gone, he joined himself to one of the citizens of that country and he sent him to his fields to feed pigs.
And he was longing to fill his belly (koilian) from out of the carob husks (pods) that the pigs were eating; and no one gave to him.
And having come unto himself, he said, How many hired servants of my father have abundance of bread, however I am here perishing with hunger?
Having risen up, I will go to my father and I will say to him, Father, I have sinned unto heaven and in your sight;
I am no longer worthy to be called your son. Make me like as one of your hired servants.
And having risen up, he went to his father, and he still being far distant, his father saw him and was moved with compassion (sympathy), and having run, fell upon his neck and fervently kissed him.
And the son said to him, Father, I have sinned unto heaven and in your sight; I am no longer worthy to be called your son. {Make me as one of your hired servants.}
And the father said to his servants, Quickly bring out the best long robe (robe worn by the upper class of the East) and clothe him and give a ring unto his hand, and sandals unto his feet;
And having brought the fattened calf, kill it, and having eaten, let us be merry (of a good outlook).
Because this son of mine was dead and is alive again; was lost and is found. And they began to be merry.

And his elder son was in the field; and while coming, he drew near to the house, he heard music (symphonias- harmony of instruments, latin-symphoniam like symphony) and dancing (choron, latin-chorum like chorus).
And having called near one of the servants, he inquired what these things might be.
And he said to him, Your brother is come and your father has killed the fattened calf, because he has received him healthy (safe and sound).
However he was angry and was not willing to go in. And his father having gone out, entreated him.

And answering, he said to his father, Behold, I serve you so many years and I never disregarded (passed over, neglected) a commandment or yours, and never to me did you give a young goat, so that my friends might make merry with me.
However when this your son, the one having devoured (eaten till it is all finished) your living with prostitutes, came, you have killed for him the fattened calf!
And he said to him, Son, you are always with me and all that is mine is yours.
And it was fitting to make merry and to rejoice because this your brother was dead and is alive again; and he was lost and is found.
And He was saying to the disciples, There was a certain rich man who had a manager and he was accused to him as wasting his possessions.
And having called him, he said to him, What is this I hear concerning you? Give an account (word) of your management; for you are no longer able to manage.
And the manager said within himself, What shall I do, because my lord is taking away the management from me? I am not able to dig; I am ashamed to beg.
I know what I will do, so that when I shall have been removed from out of the management, people might receive me into their houses.
And having called to him each one of his lord’s debtors, he said to the first, How much do you owe to my lord? gallons) of oil (thus about 800-900 gallons).
And he said, A hundred baths (one bath=8-9 gallons) of oil (thus about 800-900 gallons).� And he said to him, Take your bill (writing) and having sat down quickly write fifty.
Then to another he said, And how much do you owe? And he said, A hundred cors (one cor=120 gallons) of wheat. He says to him, Take your bill and write eighty.

And the lord praised the unrighteous manager, because he had done shrewdly (sensibly, prudently). Because the sons of this age (aionos) in this generation are more shrewd (sensible, intelligent) than the sons of light.

And I say to you, Make for yourselves friends from out of the wealth (mamona- treasure a person trusts in) of unrighteousness, so that when it fails, they might receive you into eternal tents (tabernacles).
The one who is faithful with very little, also is faithful in much; and the one who is unrighteous in very little, also is unrighteous in much.
If therefore you have not been faithful in the unrighteous wealth, who will entrust to you true [wealth]?
And if in that which is of another, you have not been faithful, who will give to you that which is yours?

No servant is able to serve two lords, for either he will hate one and will love the other; or he will be devoted (hold fast) to one, and he will despise (view down) the other. You are not able to serve God and wealth.
And the Pharisees, being lovers of money, heard all these things and they sneered (hold up nose in derision of) Him.

And He said to them, You are those justifying themselves in the sight of men, however God knows your hearts; because that which is exalted among (in) men is an abomination (reek of strench, abhorrent, detestable) in the sight of God.
The law and the prophets were until John; from that time the kingdom of God is preached (evangelized), and everyone forces his way into it.
And it is easier for the heaven and earth to pass away than one stroke of a letter of the law to fail.
Everyone divorcing (releasing/unbinding/loosing away from) his wife and marrying another commits adultery. And he marrying the one divorced away from a husband commits adultery.
And there was a certain rich man and he was clothed in purple and fine linen, making merry (good outlook) in splendor day by day.
And a certain beggar (poor man) named Lazarus was laid to his gateway being full of sores,
And passionately desiring to be feed (satisfied, filled) from the fallings from the table of the rich man; but even the dogs coming licked his sores.
And it happened that the poor man (beggar) died and he was carried away by the angels unto the bosom of Abraham. And the rich man died and was buried.
And in Hades having lifted up his eyes, being in torment, he sees Abraham from afar and Lazarus in his bosom.

And he having called out said, Father Abraham have mercy on me and send Lazarus so that he might dip (bapse) the tip of his finger in water and cool my tongue (glossan); because I am suffering painful sorrow in this flame.

And Abraham said, Child, remember that you did fully receive your good things in your life and Lazarus likewise bad (kaka) things. However now here he is entreated (comforted); and you are suffering painful sorrow.

And in [addition to] all these things, between us and you has been firmly fixed a great chasm (greek/latin-chasma), so that those wanting to pass through (pass across) from here to you are not able, nor from there to us can they pass through.
And he said, I implore you then, father, that you would send him unto the house of my father,
For I have five brothers, so that he might thoroughly testify to them, so that they also might not come unto this place of torment.
And Abraham says, They have Moses and the prophets: let them hear them.
And he said, No, father Abraham, but if one should go from the dead to them, they will repent.
And he said to him, If they do not hear Moses and the prophets, not even if one from out of the dead should rise (resurrect) will they be persuaded.
And He said to His disciples, It is impossible for the stumbling blocks not to come, however woe to him through whom they come!
It is better for him if a stone of a mill is hung around his neck and he is thrown into the seas, than that he should cause these little ones to stumble.
Take heed to yourselves: If your brother should sin, censure him; and if he should repent, remit (latin-dimitte-release) him.
And if he should sin against (unto) you seven times in a day, and should return to you seven times, saying, I repent, you will remit him.
And the apostles said to the Lord, Add faith to us!

And the Lord said, If you have faith like as a grain of mustard, you could say to this mulberry tree (greek-sykamino, latin-arbori moro-mulberry tree), Be uprooted and be plants in the sea and it would comply with you.
However which of you having a servant plowing or shepherding having come in from out of the field will say to him directly, Having come recline?
But will he not say to him, Prepare what I might dine on and having girded yourself about, serve me, until I eat and drink; and after these things will you eat and drink?
Is he not thankful to the servant, because he did the things having been commanded?
Thus you also, when you might have done all the things having been commanded you, say, We are unneeded (useless) servants, we have done that which we were obligated to do.
And it happened in the going up unto Jerusalem, He passed through the midst of Samaria and Galilee.
And on His entering into a certain village, ten leprous men met Him, who stood afar off.
And they lifted up their voice, saying, Jesus. Master-in-charge, have mercy on us.
And having seen them, He said to them, Go, show yourselves to the priests. And it happened in going to them, they were cleansed.
And one of them having seen that he was healed (iathe) turned back with a great voice; glorifying God,
and he fell upon his face beside His feet, giving thanks to Him; and he was a Samaritan.
And answering, Jesus said, Were not ten cleansed? However where are the nine?
There was none found having returned to give glory to God, except this one of another race.
And He said to him, Rise up, go forth; your faith has saved you!
And having been asked by the Pharisees, When is the kingdom of God coming? He answered them and said, The kingdom of God does not come with careful observation (watching like a guard);
Nor will they say, Behold here or there; for behold, the kingdom of God is inside (within, among) you (you is plural).
And He said to the disciples, Days will come when you will passionately long to see one of the days of the Son of Man and you will not see it.
And they will say to you, Behold there; or Behold here. Do not go after nor pursue (aggressively chase).
For just as lightning flashing shines from out of the [one end] under heaven unto the [other end] under heaven, thus will be the Son of Man is His day.
However it is first necessary for Him to suffer many things and to be rejected by (away from) this generation.
And just as it happened in the days of Noah, thus will it also be in the days of the Son of Man.
They were eating, they were drinking, they were marrying, they were being given in marriage, until that day Noah entered into the ark and the flood came and destroyed all.
Similarly, just as it happened in the days of Lot; they were eating, they were drinking, they were buying, they were selling, they were planting, they were building;
And in that day Lot went out away from Sodom, it rained fire and sulphur (brimstone- known for its purifying power, latin-sulphur) from heaven and destroyed all.
According to them will it be in that day when the Son of Man is revealed.

In that there day, he who will be upon the housetop and his goods in the house, do not let him go down to take them away; and the one in the field, likewise do not let him return unto the things behind.
Remember the wife of Lot.
Whoever might seek to preserve (fully acquire) his life (soul) will lose (destroy) it; whoever will lose (destroy) it will preserve it alive.
I say to you, In that night there will be two upon one bed (mat, couch); the one will be taken and the other will be left alone.
There will be two women grinding upon the same [place]; the one will be taken, and the other will be left alone.
{Two will be in the field; the one will we taken, and the other will be left alone.}
And answering, they say to Him, Where, Lord? And He said to them, Where the body is there also the vultures (birds of prey, eagles) will be gather together upon.
And He spoke a parable to them, to the [way] it is always necessary for them to pray and not be wearied out (faint, from out of bad, latin-deficere-fail roots are deficit/ deficient/ sink),
Saying, There was a certain judge in a certain city, not fearing God and not respecting man.
And there was a widow in that city and she was coming to him, saying, Avenge (dispense justice for) me of (away from) my adversary.
And he would not upon a time; however afterward he said within himself, If even I do not fear God nor respect man,
yet because this widow causes me trouble, I will avenge her, so that lest in the end her coming she may wear me out.
And the Lord said, Hear what the unjust (unrighteous) judge says.
And shall not God execute (do) the avenging of His elect crying to Him day and night, and is long-tempered (deferring anger) upon them?
I say to you, that He will execute (do) their avenging in quickness. Nevertheless the Son of Man having come, will He find faith upon the earth?
And He spoke this parable to some trusting upon themselves that they are righteous and despising (cast out as nothing, ignore, treat with contempt) the rest [of men]:
Two men went up into the temple to pray; the one a Pharisee and the other a tax collector.

The Pharisee having stood by himself was praying thus, God, I thank you that I am not just as the rest of men, seizers (extortioners, swindlers, robbers, greek-harpages its root word is the one for rapture, latin-raptores-abductors), unrighteous, adulterers, or even like as this tax collector.
I fast twice in week (sabbatou); I tithe all things, as many as I gain.
And the tax collector standing afar off was not willing even to lift up his eyes unto heaven, but was striking his breast, saying, God, extend propitiation (latin-propitius) to me, the sinner!
I say to you, This one went down unto his house justified rather than that [other]. Because everyone exalting himself will be humbled; however the one humbling himself will be exalted.
And the brought to Him also infants (brephe, newborn babes), so that He might touch them; however the disciples having seen censured them.
However Jesus, having called them to Him, said, Permit the little children (paidia) to come to Me and do not forbid (hinder) them; for of such is the kingdom of God.
Truly, I say to you, whoever shall not warmly receive (welcome) the kingdom of God like as a child shall not enter into it.
And a certain ruler asked Him, saying, Good Teacher, having done what will I inherit eternal life?
And Jesus said to him, Why do you call Me good? No one is good, except one, God.

You know the commandments: You should not commit adultery; you should not murder (homicide); you should not steal; you should not bear false witness; Value (honor, give honor to, assign value to) your father and mother.
And he said, I have kept all these from my youth.
And Jesus having heard said to him, Yet one thing is lacking to you: Sell all, as much as you have, and distribute to the poor and you will have treasure in the heavens; and come, follow Me.
And having heard these things, he became engulfed in sorrow; for he was exceedingly rich.
And Jesus having seen him engulfed in sorrow, said, How difficultly those having riches shall enter into the kingdom of God.
For it is easier for a camel to enter through an eye (hole) of a needle, than a rich man to enter into the kingdom of God.
And those having heard said, And who is able to be saved?
And He said, The things that are impossible (unable) with men are possible (able) with God.
And Peter said, Behold, we have left our own and followed You.
And He said to them, Truly, I say to you, There is no one who has left house, or wife, or brothers, or parents, or children for the sake of the kingdom of God,
Who shall not receive manifold (many times) more in this time; and in the age that is coming, life eternal.

And having taken the twelve to Him, He said to them, Behold, we go up unto Jerusalem, and all things having been written of the Son of Man through the prophets will be accomplished (fulfilled, completed).
For He will be betrayed to the ethnicities (Gentiles) and will be mocked and will be spitefully insulted and will be spit on.
And having flogged Him, they will kill Him; and on the third day, He will rise again.
And they understood none of these things; and this saying (rhema) was hidden from them; and they did not know what was being spoken.
And it happened in His drawing near unto Jericho, a certain blind man sat beside the road, begging.
And having heard a crowd passing by, he asked what this might be.
And they reported to him, Jesus of Nazareth is passing by.
And he called out saying, Jesus, Son of David, have mercy on me.
And those going before censured him, so that he should be silent. However he cried out much more, Son of David, have mercy on me.
And having stood still, Jesus commanded him to be brought to Him. And having drawn near, He asked pointedly of him,
What do you want Me to do to you? And he said, Lord, that I might see again.
And Jesus said to him, See again; your faith has saved you.
And instantly he saw again, and followed Him, glorifying God. And all the people having seen it gave praise to God.
And having entered, He was passing through Jericho.
And behold, a man by name called Zacchaeus, and he was a chief tax collector, and he was rich.
And he was seeking to see Jesus who He was, and he was not able because of the crowd, because he was small (little) in stature.
And having run in front of, he went up upon a sycamore tree (fig-mulberry tree), so that he might see Him, because He was about to pass that [way].
And as He came upon the place, having looked up, Jesus said to him, Zacchaeus, hurry, come down; for today it is necessary for Me to stay in your house.
And having hurried, he came down and welcomed Him as a guest, rejoicing.
And having seen it, all grumbled, saying, He has gone in to lodge with a sinful man.
And having stood, Zacchaeus said to the Lord, Behold, Lord, I give half of my possessions to the poor and if I have defrauded anything of anyone, I restore it fourfold.
And Jesus said to him, Today salvation has come to this house, because he is also a son of Abraham:
For the Son of Man came to seek and to save that having been lost.
And they were hearing these things, having proceeded, He spoke a parable, because He was near Jerusalem and they supposed that the kingdom of God was about to instantly appear.
He said therefore, A certain man of noble birth (well-born) went into a distant country to receive for himself a kingdom and return.
And having called ten of his own servants, he gave to them ten minas and said to them, Do business until I come back.
However his citizens hated him and sent a delegation after him, saying, We are not willing for this man to reign over (upon) us.

And it happened in his returning, having received the kingdom, and he said to be called to him these servants to whom he had given the money so that he might know what each had gained by doing business.
And the first came up, saying, Lord, your mina has produced ten more minas.
And he said to him, Well done, good servant! Because in very little you were faithful, be you to have authority over (upon) ten cities.
And the second came, saying, Lord, your mina has made five more minas.
And he said also to this one, And you, are to be over (upon) five cities.
And another came, saying, Lord, behold your mina, which I have lying away (laid in store) in a handkerchief.
For I feared you, because you are a harsh (severe, rigid) man; you take up what you did not lay down and you reap what you did not sow.
He says to him, Evil servant I will judge you from out of your mouth; you knew that I am a harsh man, taking up what I did not lay down and reaping what I did not sow.
And why did you not give my money to (upon) the bank and I having come, I might have collected it with interest?
And to those standing by he said, Take the mina away from him and give it to the one having the ten minas.
And they said to him, Lord, he has ten minas.
I say to you, that to everyone having will be given; and away from the one not having, even that which he has will be taken.
Moreover these enemies of mine, those having not been willing for me to reign over (upon) them, bring here and slay them before me.
And having said these things, He went on ahead, going up unto Jerusalem.
And it happened as He drew near unto Bethphage and Bethany toward the mount called Olivet, He sent two of the disciples,
Saying, Go into the village over against [you], in which entering you will find a colt having been tied, upon which no one ever yet of men has sat; and having untied it, bring it.
And if anyone asks you why do you untie it? Thus you will say, Because the Lord has need of it.
And those having been sent having departed, found it just as He had said to them.
And on their untying the colt, the lords of it said to them, Why do you untie the colt?
And they said, The Lord has need of it.
And they led it to Jesus; and having cast their garments upon the colt, they put Jesus upon it.
And as He went, they were spreading their garments on the road.

And as He drew near; already at the descent of the mount of Olives, all the multitude of the disciples began, rejoicing, to praise God with a great voice concerning all the miracles which they had seen,
Saying, Blessed is the King coming in the name of the Lord. Peace in heaven, and glory in the highest (latin-gloria in excelsis).
And some of the Pharisees from the crowd said to Him, Teacher, censure Your disciples.
And answering, He said, I say to you, If these will be silent, the stones will cry out.
And as He drew near, having seen the city, He wept over (upon) it,
Saying, If you had known in this day, even you, the things which make for peace, however now they are hidden from your eyes.
Because the days will come upon you and your enemies will cast a barricade around you; and will surround you and will hem (press and hold) you in on every side,

and will level you and your children to the ground within you, and will not leave a stone upon a stone within you, because you did not know the opportune time of your visitation (oversight, visitation of judgment, scoping upon, latin-visitationis-visitation).
And having entered into the temple, He began to cast out those selling,
Saying to them, It has been written and My house will be a house of prayer; however you have made it a den (cave) of robbers (thieves in the open).
And He was teaching day by day in the temple; however the chief priests and the scribes and the foremost (principal, first) of the people were seeking to destroy Him,
And they did not find what they might do; for all the people were hanging on His [words] listening.
And it happened on one of the days as He was teaching the people in the temple and preaching good news, the chief priests and the scribes with the elders came up.
And they spoke saying to Him, Tell us by (in) what authority You do these things, or who is the one having given this authority to You?
And answering, He said to them, I also will ask you a word, and you tell Me:
The baptism of John, was it from out of heaven or from out of men?
And they reckoned together among themselves, saying, If we should say, From out of heaven, He will say, Why did you not believe him?
However if we should say, From out of men, all the people will stone us; for they are persuaded that John was a prophet.
And they answered, They did not know from where.
And Jesus said to them, Neither will I tell you by (in) what authority I am doing these things.
And He began to speak to the people this parable: A certain man planted a vineyard and gave it out for hire to vinedressers (latin-colonis-settlers) and left the country a considerable time.

And in the opportune time (fitting season), he sent a servant to the vinedressers, so that they will give to him from the fruit of the vineyard. However the vinedressers having beaten him, sent him away empty-handed.
And he proceeded to send another servant; however having beaten and dishonored him, they sent him away empty-handed.
And he proceeded to send a third; however also having wounded (greek is root for English traumatized) him, they cast him out.
And the lord of the vineyard said, What shall I do? I will send my beloved son; perhaps they will reverence (respect) him.
The vinedressers having seen him dialogued among themselves, saying, This is the heir; let us kill him so that the inheritance might become ours.
And having cast him outside the vineyard, they killed him. What therefore will the lord of the vineyard do to them?
He will come and destroy these vinedressers and will give the vineyard to others. And having heard it, they said, May it never be!
However having looked at them, He said, What then is this that has been written, The stone which those building rejected, this has become unto the head of the corner?
Everyone falling upon that stone will be pulverized (broken in pieces, crushed together), and upon whomever it might fall, it will crush him to powder.

And the chief priests and scribes sought to throw (strike) their hands upon Him in the same hour; and they feared the people; for they knew that He was speaking this parable against (toward, to) them.

And having watched Him closely, they sent spies pretending themselves to be righteous (just), so that they might catch a word of Him, so that they might hand Him over to the rule and authority of the governor.

And they questioned him, saying, Teacher, we know that You speak and teach rightly (straightly, correctly), and do not receive the face (of a person), but teach the way of God upon (upon the basis of) truth.
Is it permitted for us to give tribute (tax) to Caesar or not?
However having perceived their craftiness, He said to them,
Show me a denarius: Whose image and inscription has it? And they said, Caesar’s.
And He said to them, Now then give from the things of Caesar to Caesar, and the things of God to God.
And they were not able to catch Him in His saying (rhematos) before the people; and having marveled upon His answer, they became silent.
And some of the Sadducees, denying there is a resurrection, having come to Him, they questioned Him,
Saying, Teacher, Moses wrote to us, If anyone’s brother having a wife should die, and he is childless, that his brother should take the wife, and should raise up seed to his brother.
There were then seven brothers; and the first, having taken a wife, died childless;
And the second {as the wife, and he died childless};
And the third took her; and likewise also the seven, did not leave children, and died.
Lastly the woman also died.
The wife therefore, in the resurrection, of which of them does she become wife? For the seven had her as wife.
And Jesus said to them, The sons of this age (aionos) marry and are given in marriage;
However those having been considered worthy to obtain to that there age and the resurrection from out of the dead, neither marry nor are given in marriage;
For they are not able to die anymore: for they are identical to the angels (isangeloi- isos means equal, identical, equivalent); and are sons of God, being sons of the resurrection.
And because the dead are raised, even Moses made known (revealed, informed) upon the bush, when he calls the Lord the God of Abraham, and God of Isaac, and God of Jacob;
And He is not God of the dead, but of the living; for all live to Him.
And answering, some of the scribes said, Teacher, you have spoken well (kalos).
For they no longer dared to ask Him anything.
And He said to them, How do they say that the Christ be the son of David?
For David himself says in the book of Psalms, The Lord said to my lord, Sit at (from out of) My right,
Until I place (put, set) Your enemies as a footstool of Your feet.
David therefore calls Him Lord and how is He his Son?
And all the people were listening, He said to His disciples,
Beware of the scribes, wanting to walk in long robes and loving greetings in the marketplaces, and the chief seats in the synagogues, and chief places in the banquets (suppers);
Who devour the houses of widows and as a pretense pray at great length. These will receive an excessive (greater, more abundant, exceeding) condemnation.
And having looked up, He saw the rich casting their gifts into the treasury;
And He saw a certain poor (needy, one that works for their daily need) widow casting in there two lepta (small very low value copper coin).
And He said, Truly (Of truth) I say to you, that this poor (beggarly, destitute) widow cast in more than all;
For all these cast in gifts from out of that which was abounding; however she from out of her poverty cast in all the livelihood that she had.
And as some were speaking about the temple, that it was adorned with goodly (beautiful, greek-kalios) stones and consecrated offerings, He said,
These things which you are beholding, Days will come in which there will not be left (same word for remit) stone upon stone {here} which will not be thrown down.
And they asked Him, saying, Teacher, when then will these things be, and what is the sign when these things are about to happen?
And He said, Take heed (see) lest you be led astray; for many will come upon My name, saying, I am [he], and the time (opportune/fitting time) is drawn near. Do not go after (behind) them.

And when you should hear of wars and instabilities (not-down-standing or not standing down, disturbances, commotions, uprisings, insurrections, latin-seditiones), do not be terrified; for it is necessary for these things to first happen, but the end is not directly (at once, immediately).
Then He was saying to them, Ethnicity (nation) will rise up (awaken, arouse) against (upon) ethnicity (nation), and kingdom against (upon) kingdom;

Great earthquakes, and in places by places (kai kata topous) famines and epidemics (pestilences, pesty things like diseases, plagues, and such, latin-pestilantiae-epidemics). There will be fearful sights (causes of terror, terrors) and there also will be great signs from (away from) heaven.

However (and) before all these things, they will throw their hands upon you and will persecute you, handing you over unto the synagogues and prisons, taking (leading) you away before (upon) kings and governors, on account of (for the sake of) My name.
It will result to you unto a testimony.
Set (Place, Put, Lay) therefore in your hearts not to premeditate to make a defense;
For I will give you a mouth and wisdom, which none of all those opposing you will be able to resist (stand against, oppose, withstand), nor speak opposition against.
And you will be betrayed (handed over) even by parents and brothers and relatives and friends; and they will put to death [some] from among you.
And you will be hated (detested) by all because of My name.
And a hair of your head should not perish.
In your endurance you will acquire (gain, get) your lives (souls).
And when you see Jerusalem being encircled by (under) armies (encamped military), then know that her desolation (close to the word for wilderness/ desert/ desolate place) has drawn near.
Then those in Judea, let them flee unto the mountains; and those in her midst (middle), let them depart out; and those in the countries (regions), do not let them enter into her;
Because these are days of avenging, all things having been written are to be accomplished (fulfilled).

But woe to those having [a child] in the womb, and to those nursing in those days; for there will be great distress (necessity, constraint, a pressing situation, latin-pressura-pressure) upon the land and wrath to this people.

And they will fall by the mouth of the sword (slaughter-dagger) and will be led captive into all the nations (ethnicities); and Jerusalem will be tread on (trampled) by the ethnicities (nations) until the times of the ethnicities (nations) be fulfilled.

And there will be signs in the sun, and moon, and stars; and upon the earth distress (tension from difficult circumstances) of ethnicities (nations) with perplexity (to be at a loss because of difficulties), the roaring (noise, sound) of the sea and tossing of the sea in a tempest,

And men are fainting (losing morale, losing soul/breathe, dismaying) from fear and expectation of that which is coming upon the inhabited world; for the powers of the heavens will be shaken (agitated, latin-movebuntur-moved).
And then (at that time) they will see the Son of Man coming in a cloud with power and much glory.
And these when things begin (commence) to happen, look up and lift up your heads, because your redemption draws near.
And He spoke a parable to them: Behold the fig tree and all the trees:
When they already sprout (throw before), looking at them of (away from) yourselves, you know that summer is already near.
So also you, when you see these things happening, know that the kingdom of God is near.
Truly I say to you, that this generation will not have passed away until all things shall have happened.
The heaven and the earth will pass away, however My words will not pass away.

And take heed to yourselves, lest ever your hearts be weighed down (burdened) with drunken dissipation (kraipale, drunken nausea, what seizes you from drunkenness, a headache from drunkenness, latin-crapula-carousing root is reeling) and drunkenness, and cares (anxieties) of life; and that there day would come (befall, stand, set) upon you suddenly (unexpectedly);
For like as a snare it will come upon all those sitting upon all the surface (face) of the earth.
And watch in every season (opportune/fitting time), appealing that you might have strength to escape all these things that are about to happen and to stand before the Son of Man.
And by day He was day teaching in the temple and by night, going out, he lodged on the mount called Olivet.
And all the people came early in the morning to Him in the temple to hear Him.
And the feast of Unleavened Bread, called Passover (pascha), drew near.
And the chief priests and scribes were seeking how they might make an end of Him (take Him away); for they feared the people.
And Satan entered into Judas called Iscariot, being from out of the number of the twelve.
And having gone away, he spoke with the chief priests and captains on how he might betray (hand over) Him to them.
And they rejoiced and agreed to give him money.
And he fully consented (confessed fully) and sought a good opportunity (convenient time, a good opportune time) to betray Him apart from the crowd.
And the day of Unleavened Bread came on which it was necessary for the Passover lamb to be killed.
And He sent Peter and John, having said, Go prepare the Passover for us, so that we might eat it.
And they said to Him, Where do You want us to prepare it?
And He said to them, Behold, on you having entered into the city, a man will meet you carrying a pitcher of water; follow him into the house into which he enters;
And you will say to the master of the house, The Teacher says to you, Where is the guest room where I might eat the Passover with My disciples?
And he will show you a great upper room having been furnished (spread); there prepare.
And having gone, they found it just as He had said to them; and they prepared the Passover.
And when the hour was come, He reclined [at the table] and the apostles with Him.
And He said to them, Upon passionate longing I have passionately longed to eat this Passover with you before I suffer.
For I say to you, that I will not eat of it any longer, until when it is fulfilled in the kingdom of God.
And having received the cup, having given thanks, He said, Take this, and divide it among yourselves.

For I say to you, that I will not drink from now from the produce (not karpos-fruit but rather genematos which means offspring or that begotten/birthed) of the vine until the kingdom of God shall come.
And having taken the bread, having given thanks, He broke and gave to them, saying, This is My body, which is given for you; this do in My remembrance.
And the cup likewise after which having supped (eaten at supper time), saying, This cup is the new covenant in My blood, which is being poured out from you.
However, behold, the hand of him betraying Me is with Me upon the table;
Because the Son of Man indeed goes according to that having been determined, however woe to that there man through whom He is betrayed.
And they began to examine this together among themselves, who then it might be from out of them, who is about to do this.
And there was also a fondness of dispute (love of strife/contention) among them, as to which of them is supposed to be the greatest.
And He said to them, The kings of the ethnicities rule over them and those exercising authority over them are called benefactors (well-workers).
And you shall not be thus; but the greatest among you, let him be as the younger; and the one leading as the one serving.
For who is greater, the one reclining or the one serving? Is not the one reclining? However I am in the midst of you, as the One serving.
And you are those having remained with Me in My trials.
And I appoint to you, just as My Father appointed to Me, a kingdom;
So that you might eat and might drink at (upon) My table in My kingdom; and are to sit upon thrones, judging the twelve tribes of Israel.
Simon, Simon, Behold, Satan has asked you out for himself, to sift as wheat;
however I appealed (requested an urgent appeal) concerning you, so that you faith might not fail; and you, when you have turned back (turned/changed/converted upon), firmly confirm your brothers.
And he said to Him, Lord, I am ready to go with You unto prison and unto death.
And He said, I tell you Peter, the rooster will not crow (call out) today until you will deny three times not knowing Me.
And He said to them, When I sent you without a money-bag, and traveler’s bag, and sandals, did you lack anything? And they said, Nothing.

He said therefore to them, But now the one having the money-bag let him take it likewise also a traveler’s bag; and the one not having a sword (slaughter-dagger), let him sell his outer garment and buy one;
For I say to you, that this that has been written must be accomplished in Me, And He was reckoned with the lawless; for also the things concerning Me have an end (a fulfillment).
And they said, Lord, behold, here are two swords (slaughter-daggers). And He said to them, It is enough (sufficient, fit, suitable).
And having gone out, He went according to custom unto the mount of Olives; and the disciples also followed Him.
And having come upon the place, He said to them, Pray that you do not enter into temptation.
And He withdrew away from them about a stone’s throw and having fallen on His knees, He prayed,
Saying, Father, if You are willing take this cup away from Me; however not My will, but of You be done.
And an angel appeared to Him from heaven, strengthening Him.
And having been in agony, He prayed more intently. And His sweat became as large drops of blood falling down upon the ground.
And having risen up from prayer, having come to the disciples, He found them sleeping from grief (pain, distress, vexation),
and He said to them, Why are you sleeping? Rise up and pray, so that you might not enter into temptation.
As He was still speaking, behold, a crowd, and he who is called Judas, one of the twelve, was going before them, and drew near to Jesus to kiss Him.
And Jesus said to him, Judas, are you betraying the Son of Man with a kiss?
And those around Him having seen what will be, said Lord, will we strike with the sword?
And a certain one of them struck the servant of the high priest and cut off his right ear.
And answering, Jesus said, Permit them as far as this! And having touched the ear, He healed (iasato) him.

And Jesus said to those having arrived against (upon) Him, the chief priests, and captains of the temple, and elders, Have you come out as against (upon) a robber (one that steals in the open) with swords and sticks (things of wood)?
My being with you day by day in the temple, you did not stretch out your hands against (upon) Me; but this is your hour and the authority of darkness.
And having apprehended Him, they led Him away and led Him into the house of the high priest. And Peter was following afar off.
And they having kindled a fire in the midst of the courtyard, and having sat down together, Peter was sitting in the midst of them.
And having seen him, a certain servant girl sitting by the light, and having looked fixedly (fixated) on him, said, This one also was with Him.
However he denied it, saying, Woman, I do not know Him.
And after a little, another having seen him, said, You are also from out of them. However Peter said, Man, I am not.
And about one hour having elapsed, a certain other strongly asserted it, saying, Upon truth this one also was with Him; for he is a Galilean.
And Peter said, Man, I do not know what you say. And instantly while he was yet speaking, the rooster crowed (called out).
And having turned, the Lord looked at Peter; and Peter remembered the word of the Lord, how He said to him, Before the rooster crows today, you will deny Me three times.
And having gone outside, he wept bitterly.
And the men who are holding (confining) Him mocked Him, beating Him.
And having blindfolded (covered around) Him, {they were striking His face, and} were asking saying, Prophesy, who is the one having struck you?
And they said unto Him many other things blaspheming.
And when it became day, the elderhood of the people, both the chief priests and scribes were gathered together and they led Him into their council, saying,
If You are the Christ, tell us. And He said to them, If I should tell you, you would not believe;
And if I should ask you, you would not answer.
From now on the Son of Man will be sitting at (from out of) the right hand/side of the power of God.
And they all said, You then are the Son of God? And to them He said, You say that I am.
And they said, What need have we of any more witness? For we ourselves have heard it from His mouth.
And the whole multitude of them having risen up led Him to (upon) Pilate.
And they began to accuse Him, saying, We found this Man misleading our nation (ethnicity) and forbidding tribute (tax) to be given to Caesar, and saying He Himself to be Christ, a King.
And Pilate questioned Him, saying, Are You the king of the Jews? And answering him, He said, You say.
And Pilate said to the chief priests and the crowds, I find no cause (guilt, fault) in this man.
However they were growing stronger (insisting, strong upon), saying, He stirs up the people, teaching throughout the whole of Judea, and He has begun from Galilee even to here.
And Pilate having heard, asked whether the Man is a Galilean;
And having recognized that He is from the jurisdiction (authority) of Herod, he sent Him up to Herod, being himself also in Jerusalem in those days.
And Herod, having seen Jesus was very glad; for he was for a considerable time wanting to see Him, because of hearing concerning Him; and he was hoping to see some sign done by Him.
And he questioned Him in many words; however He answered him nothing.
And the chief priests and the scribes had been standing by vigorously accusing Him.
And Herod with his troops treated Him as nothing, and mocked Him, put splendid clothing on Him, he sent Him back to Pilate.
And Pilate and Herod became friends with one another on the same day; for before they were at (in) enmity between themselves.
And Pilate having called together the chief priests, and the rulers, and the people,

Said to them, You brought this Man to me as misleading the people; and behold, I having examined Him in your sight, found nothing causative in this man of that accusation you are bringing against Him.
Nor did Herod; for he sent Him back to us. And behold, nothing worthy of death is done (regularly practiced) by Him.
Having therefore chastised (disciplined as a child) Him, I will release Him.
{and of necessity he had to release one at the feast.}
However they cried out all together, saying, Away (remove) with this Man, release Barabbas to us!
Who was cast into prison on account of a certain insurrection having been made in the city and murder.
Pilate therefore called again to them wanting to release Jesus.
However they were calling out (shouting), saying, Crucify, crucify Him.
And a third time he said to them, For what wrong did this Man commit? I found no cause of death in Him. Having therefore chastised Him, I will release Him.
And they were insistent (set upon) with great voices, demanding Him to be crucified. And their voices {and of the chief priests} prevailed (overpowered, strong against).
And Pilate sentenced their demand to be done.
And he released the one having been cast into prison on account of insurrection and murder, whom they had asked for; and he handed over Jesus to their will.
And as they led Him away, having laid hold upon Simon, a certain of Cyrene, coming from the country, they put the cross upon him to carry it behind Jesus.
And a great multitude of the people and of women who were mourning and lamenting for Him were following.
And having turned to them, Jesus said, Daughters of Jerusalem, do not weep for (upon) Me, however weep for (upon) yourselves and for (upon) your children.
Because behold, days are coming in which they will say, Blessed are the barren and the wombs that never did bear, and breasts that never nursed.
Then will they begin to say to the mountains, Fall upon us; and to the hills, Cover (conceal) us:
For if these things they do in the sap-filled tree, what might happen in the dry?
And also two other criminals (wrong-workers) were being led away with Him to be put to death.

And when they came upon the place called The Skull (greek-Kranion, Latin-Calvariae, KJV-Calvary), there they crucified Him and the criminals, one on (from out of) the right, and one on (from out of) the left.
And Jesus said, Father, remit them, for they do not know what they do. And dividing his garments (outer garments), they cast a lot.
And the people stood beholding. And the rulers were sneering at (deriding) Him, saying, He saved others, let Him save Himself if this is the Christ, the Chosen [One] (Elect) of God.
And the soldiers also mocked Him, coming near, offering Him sour wine vinegar,
And saying, If You are the King of the Jews, save Yourself!
And there was also an inscription over (upon) Him, This is the King of the Jews, in Greek and Latin and Hebrew letters.
And one of the criminals having been suspended, blasphemed Him saying, Are You not the Christ? Save Yourself and us!
And answering, the other was censuring him, saying, Do you not fear God, because you are in the same judgment?
And we indeed justly, for we are receiving worthy of what we did (regularly practiced); however this Man did (practiced) nothing out of place (atopon).
And he said to Jesus, Remember me when You come into Your kingdom!
And He said to him, Truly to you, I say, you will be with Me today in Paradise.
And it was already about the sixth hour and darkness came over (upon) the whole land until the ninth hour.

The sun was obscured (leave off, eklipontos- root of English word for eclipsed, latin-obscuratus-darkened roots are obscured/ minimizing), and the veil of the temple was divided asunder in the middle.
And having called out with a great voice, Jesus said, Father into Your hands I commit My spirit. And having said this, He breathed out His last.
And the centurion having seen what happened, glorified God, saying, Certainly this Man was righteous (just).
And all the crowds having come together upon this spectacle, having beheld the things having happened, returned [home] beating their breasts (chests).
And all those who knew Him stood from afar off also the women who followed Him from Galilee, seeing these things.
And behold, a man named Joseph, a Council member, being a good and righteous man,
(He had not consented to their counsel/resolved plan and action), from Arimathaea, a city of the Jews, who was waiting for the kingdom of God.
He having gone to Pilate, asked for the body of Jesus.
And having taken it down, he wrapped it in a fine linen cloth, and placed it in a tomb cut in a rock, in which no one had been yet laid.
And it was the day of preparation (the day before the Sabbath-Friday), and the Sabbath was coming on.
And the women who had come with Him from out of Galilee, having followed, beheld the tomb and how His body was placed.
And having returned, they prepared aromatic spices and anointing ointments and rested (remained tranquil) on the Sabbath according to the commandment.
And the first day of the week, very early morning (deep early dawn/day break), they came upon the tomb, bringing the aromatic spices that they had prepared,
And they found the stone having been rolled away from the tomb.
And having entered, they did not find the body of the Lord Jesus.
And it happened while they were perplexed about it, and behold, two men stood by (stood upon) them in dazzling (flashing, lightening, word for star at root) clothing.
And having become full of fear (terrified) of them, and bowing their faces unto the ground, they said to them, Why do you seek the living among (with, after) the dead?
He is not here, but rather is risen! Remember how He spoke to you, yet being in Galilee,
Saying, The Son of Man must be handed over into the hands of sinful men and be crucified and arise the third day.
And they remembered His sayings (rhematon).
And having returned from the tomb, they announced (reported) these things to all the eleven and to all the rest.
And it was Mary Magdalene, and Joanna, and Mary the [mother] of James, and the rest with them who told these things to the apostles.
And their sayings (rhemata) appeared in their sight like nonsense (idle/silly talk), and they did not believe them.

However Peter having risen up ran to (upon) the tomb and having stooped down, he sees only the fine linen wrappings and he went away, wondering at that having happened to himself. miles) from Jerusalem, whose name is Emmaus;
And behold, two of them, on the same day, were going unto a village being distant, sixty stadias (stadia-one eighth of a Roman mile, about 7 miles) from Jerusalem, whose name is Emmaus;
And they were conversing with one another concerning all these things having occurred.
And it happened in their conversing and inquiring together, and Jesus Himself having drawn near went with them;
However their eyes were held so as not to recognize Him.

And He said to them, What are these words that you exchange (throw against each other) with one another while walking? And they stood still, looking gloomy-eyed (downcast, sad, sullen-eyed, latin-tristes-sad).
And answering, the one named Cleopas said to Him, Are you alone visiting Jerusalem and do not know the things having happened in it in these days?
And He said to them, What things? And he said to Him, the things concerning Jesus of Nazareth, who became a man, a Prophet powerful in deed and word before God and all the people;
That the chief priests and our rulers handed Him over unto judgment of death and crucified Him.
And we were hoping it was He, who is about to redeem Israel. But indeed also with all these things, brings us to this third day from which these things happened.
But also certain women from out of us astonished us. Having been early to (upon) the tomb,
And having not found His body, they came declaring to have seen a vision (latin-visionem) of angels who say He is alive.
And some of those with us went to (upon) the tomb and found it so, just as the women said; and they did not see Him.
And He said to them, O non-thinking and slow of heart to believe upon all that the prophets spoke.
Was it not necessary for the Christ to suffer these things? And to enter into His glory?
And having begun from Moses and from all the prophets, He thoroughly interpreted to them in all the Scriptures the things concerning Himself.
And they drew near unto the village where they were going and He acted as though He was going farther.
And they compelled Him, saying, Abide with us, because it is toward evening and the day has already declined. And He entered in to abide with them.
And it happened in His reclining with them, having taken the bread He blessed, and having broken, He gave it to them.
And their eyes were fully opened and they recognized Him. And He became unapparent (invisible) to (away from) them.
And they said to one another, Was not our heart burning within us as He was speaking with us on the way as He was fully opening the Scriptures to us?
And having risen up that same hour, they returned unto Jerusalem and they found the eleven and those with them gathered together,
Saying, indeed, the Lord has risen and has appeared to Simon.
And they related the things on the way and how He was known to them in the breaking of the bread.
And as they were saying these things to them, He Himself stood in the midst of them and says to them, Peace to you.
However having been startled (fluttered, alarmed) and having been filled with fear, they supposed to behold a spirit.
And He said to them, Why are you troubled (agitated, stirred up)? And why do deliberations come up in your hearts?
See My hands and My feet, that I am He. Touch (Feel) Me and see, because a spirit has not flesh and bones, just as you see Me having.
And having said this, He showed to them the hands and the feet.
And still while they were not believing for (away from) joy and were marveling, He said to them, Do you have anything edible here?
And they gave to Him a part of a broiled (roasted) fish {KJV, Darby, YLT add- and of honeycomb}.
And having taken it, He ate it in their sight.

And He said to them, These are My words, which I spoke to you still being with you, because it is necessary for all things having been written in the law of Moses, and the prophets and psalms concerning Me to be fulfilled.
Then He opened their mind (understanding) to synthesize (understand, put together) the Scriptures,
And said to them, This it has been written, the Christ was to suffer, and to rise up from out of the dead the third day;
And repentance and remission of sins to be preached upon His name unto all ethnicities (nations) having begun from Jerusalem.
You are witnesses of these things.
And behold, I am sending out the promise of My Father upon you; and you remain in the city until that you should be endued (put on, clothed) with power from out of on high.
And He led them outside as far as to Bethany and having lifted up His hands, He blessed them.
And it happened in His blessing them, He was separated away from them and was carried up (brought up, led up) into heaven.
And they having worshipped Him, returned unto Jerusalem with great joy.
And all were continually in the temple, blessing God.
In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and God was the Word.
He was in the beginning with God.
All things came into being through Him and without Him not even one came into being that has come into being.
In Him was life and the life was the light of men.
And the light shines in the darkness and the darkness did not overtake (apprehend, seize) it.
There was a man having been sent from God, his name John.
He came for a witness, so that he might testify concerning the Light, so that all might believe through him.
He was not the Light, but so that he might witness concerning the Light.
The true Light was that which enlightens every man coming into the world.
He was in the world and the world came into being through Him and the world did not know Him.
He came unto His own, and His own did not receive Him;
However as many as received Him, He gave to them authority to be children of God, to those believing unto His name,
Who were born not from out of bloods, nor from out of the will of flesh, nor from out of the will of man, but from out of God.
And the Word became flesh and tabernacle among (in) us, and we beheld (we spectated) His glory, a glory as of an only-begotten from (from close beside) the Father, full of grace and truth.
John witnesses concerning Him and cried out, saying, This was He of whom I was saying, The One coming after me has precedence of me for He was before (foremost, first) me.
Because from out of His fullness, we have all received grace over-against (in place of, corresponding to, on top of) grace.
Because the law was given through Moses; grace and truth came through Jesus Christ.
No one has ever yet seen God; the only-begotten God, the One being in the bosom of the Father, He has made Him known (explained, word for exegete).
And this is the testimony of John, when the Jews sent priests and Levites from out of Jerusalem, so that they might ask him, Who are you?
And he confessed and did not deny, and confessed, I am not the Christ.
And they asked him, What then? Are you Elijah? And he says, I am not. Are you the prophet? And he answered, No.
They then said to him, Who are you? So that we might give an answer to those having sent us: What do you say about yourself?
He said, I [am] a voice crying (shouting/calling out with intense feeling, exclaiming) in the wilderness, Make straight the way of the Lord, just as Isaiah the prophet said.
And those having been sent were from out of the Pharisees.
And they asked him and said to him, Why then do you baptize if you are not the Christ, nor Elijah, nor the prophet?
John answered them saying, I baptize in (with) water; One stands in the midst of you whom you do not know,
The One coming after me of whom I am not worthy that I should untie (loose) the strap of his sandal.
These things happened in Bethany across the Jordan where John was baptizing.
On the next day he sees Jesus coming to him and says, Behold the Lamb of God taking away the sins of the world.
It is He by (under) whom I said, After me comes a man who has precedence of me, because He was before me.
And I did not know Him, but that He might be made apparent to Israel, because of this, I came baptizing in water.
And John bore witness saying, I have beheld (spectated) the Spirit descending like as a dove from out of heaven and it remained upon Him.
And I did not know him; but the One having sent me to baptize in water, He said to me, Upon whom you shall see the Spirit descending and abiding upon Him, He is the One baptizing in the Holy Spirit.
And I have seen (perceived as in a vision) and have borne witness that this is the Son of God.
On the next day John was standing again and two from out of his disciples.
And having looked at Jesus walking, he says, Behold the Lamb of God!
And the two disciples heard him speaking and followed Jesus.
However Jesus having turned and having beheld them following said to them, What do you seek? And they said to him, Rabbi- which being translated is to say, Teacher—where are you staying?
He says to them, Come and you will see. So they went and saw where He was staying; and they stayed with him that day. It was about the tenth hour.
Andrew the brother of Simon Peter was one of the two having heard this from John and having followed him.
He first finds his own brother Simon and says to him, We have found the Messiah (greek-Messian, latin-messiam), which is translated Christ.
He led him to Jesus. Jesus having looked at him said, You are Simon the son of Jonah; you will be called Cephas (Aramaic for rock), which interprets as Peter (Greek for stone).
On the next day He wanted to go forth into Galilee and He finds Philip. And Jesus says to him, Follow (accompany) me.
And Philip was from Bethsaida from out of the city of Andrew and Peter.
Philip finds Nathanael and says to him, We have found Him of whom Moses wrote of in the law, and the prophets, Jesus son of Joseph who is from Nazareth.
And Nathanael said to him, Is any good thing able to be from out of Nazareth? Philip says to him, Come and see.
Jesus saw Nathanael coming to Him and He says concerning him, Behold truly an Israelite in whom is no deceit (deceit by guile).
Nathanael says to Him, From where do you know me? Jesus answered and said to him, Before Philip called you, when being under the fig tree, I saw you.
Nathanael answered Him, Rabbi, You are the Son of God, You are King of Israel.
Jesus answered and said to him, Because I said to you, I saw you under the fig tree, do you believe? You will see (perceive as in a vision) greater things than these.
And He says to him, Truly (amen), truly (amen) I say to you, you will see (envision) the heaven opened and the angels of God ascending and descending upon the Son of Man.
And on the third day a marriage happened in Cana of Galilee and the mother of Jesus was there.
And also Jesus was invited and His disciples unto the marriage.
And having been deficient of wine (oinou), Jesus’ mother says to Him, They do not have wine.
And Jesus says to her, Woman, What to Me and to you? My hour is not yet come.
His mother says to the servants (diakonois), Whatever He might say to you, do. gallons or 39.39 liters).
Now there were lying six waterpots of stone according to the purification of the Jews having space for two or three metretae ( a liquid measure containing 8.75 gallons or 39.39 liters).
Jesus says to them, Fill full (load, greek-gemisate) the waterpots with water. And they were filled them full as far as the top (brim, above, up, latin-summum-top).
And He says to them, Draw out now and carry to the banquet-superintendent (ruler/head of a dining room with three couches). And they carried it.

And when the banquet-superintendent had tasted the water having become wine and did not know from where it is, however the servants having drawn the water knew, the banquet-superintendent calls the bridegroom (nymphion, latin-sponsum-spouse),

And says to him, Every man places first the good (kalon-winsomely good) wine and when they might have become drunk (methysthosin- common word for being intoxicated, latin-inebriati-intoxicated like inebriated), then the inferior (lesser, smaller); you have kept the good wine until just now.
This beginning of the signs Jesus did in Cana of Galilee, and made apparent His glory; and His disciples believed in (unto) Him.
After this He went down unto Capernaum, He and His mother and His brothers and His disciples and there they stayed not many days.
And the Passover (pascha-the feast of Passover) of the Jews was near and Jesus went up unto Jerusalem.
And He found in the temple those selling oxen and sheep and doves and the money-changers sitting.
And having made a whip from out of cords, He drove out all from the temple, both sheep and oxen; and He poured out the coins of the money-changers, and overturned the tables.
And to those selling doves, He said, Take these things from here; Do not make the house of My Father a house of trade (emporiou like an emporium, a marketplace for trading).
His disciples remembered that it is written, The zeal of Your house will consume Me.
Then the Jews answered and said to Him, What sign do You show to us that You do these things?
Jesus answered and said to them, Destroy this temple and in three days I will raise it up.
Then the Jews said, Forty and six years was this temple built and You will raise it up in three days?
However He spoke concerning the temple of His body.
When therefore He was raised up from out of the dead, His disciples remembered that He had said this and believed the Scripture and the word that Jesus had spoken.
And when He was in Jerusalem in the Passover, at the Feast, many believed unto His name, beholding His signs which He was doing.
However Jesus Himself did not entrust Himself to them, because of His knowing all [men].
And that He had no need that any should testify concerning man, for He knew what was in man.
And there was a man from out of the Pharisees, his name Nicodemus, a ruler of the Jews;
He came to Him by night and said to Him, Rabbi, we know that You have come from God, a teacher; for no one is able to do these signs that You do unless God should be with him.
Jesus answered and said to him, Truly (Amen), truly (amen) I say to you, If anyone be not born from above, he is not able to see the kingdom of God.
Nicodemus says to Him, How is a man able to be born, being old? Is he able to enter a second time into the womb of his mother and be born?
Jesus answered, Truly, truly, I say to you, If anyone be not born from out of water and Spirit, he is not able to enter into the kingdom of God.
That having been born from out of the flesh is flesh; and that having been born from out of the Spirit is spirit.
Do not marvel that I said to you, It is necessary for you to be born from above.

The wind (Spirit) blows (breathes) where it (He) wants and the sound (voice) of it (Him) you hear, but know not from where it (He) comes and where it (He) goes; thus is everyone having been born from out of the Spirit.
Nicodemus answered and said to Him, How are these things able to be?
Jesus answered and said to him, You are the teacher of Israel and you do not know these things?
Truly, truly I say to you, We speak that that which we know, and we bear witness of that which we have seen; and you do not receive our witness.
If I have told the earthly things to you and you do not believe, how, if I tell the heavenly things to you, will you believe?
And no one has gone up into heaven, unless the One from out of heaven having come down, the Son of man, {who is in heaven}.
And just as Moses lifted up the serpent in the wilderness, thus the Son of man must be lifted up,
So that everyone believing in Him might have life eternal.
For God thus (in this way) loved the world that He gave His only begotten Son, so that everyone believing unto Him should not perish but might have life eternal.
For God did not send His Son into the world so that He might judge the world, but so that the world might be saved through Him.
The one believing unto Him is not judged; however the one not believing has already been judged because he has not believed unto the name of the only begotten Son of God.
And this is the judgment, that light has come into the world and men loved the darkness rather than the light; for their works were evil (ponera).
For everyone practicing evil things (phaula, foul, worthless, bad) hates (detests) the light and does not come to the light, so that his works might be exposed (reproved, convicted, rebuked);
However the one doing the truth comes to the light, so that his works might be made apparent that they have been done in God.
After these things, Jesus and His disciples came into the Judean land; and there He stayed with them, and was baptizing.
And John was also baptizing in Aenon, near Salim, because there were many waters there; and they were coming and being baptized.
For John had not yet been cast into prison.
Then arose a question from out of the disciples of John with a Jew concerning purification.
And they came to John and said to him, Rabbi, He who was with you beyond the Jordan to whom you have borne witness, behold He baptizes and all come to Him.
John answered and said, A man is not able to receive anything unless it is given to him from out of heaven.
You yourselves bear witness to me that I said that I am not the Christ, but that I am sent before Him.

The one having the bride (nymphen) is the bridegroom (nymphios); however the friend of the bridegroom, the one standing and hearing him, rejoices with with because of the voice of the bridegroom, this then, the joy of mine is fulfilled.
That One there must increase (grow, become greater), however me decrease (make less in rank or influence, make inferior).
The One coming from above is above all. The one being from out of the earth is from out of the earth and speaks from out of the earth. The One coming from heaven is above all.
What He has seen and heard, this He testifies; and no one receives His testimony.
The one having received His testimony has set his seal to this, that God is true;
For He whom God sent speaks the sayings (rhemata) of God; for He gives the Spirit not from out of measure [to Him].
The Father loves the Son and has given all things in His hand.
The one believing unto the Son has life eternal; and the one refusing to be persuaded of the Son will not see life, but the wrath of God remains upon him.
When therefore Jesus knew that the Pharisees heard that Jesus makes and baptizes more disciples than John,
