And I heard a great voice from out of heaven, saying, Behold, the tabernacle of God is with men and He will tabernacle with them and they will be His peoples (of note-people is plural here), and God Himself will be with them as their God.
And He will wipe away every tear from out of their eyes; and death will be no longer, nor mourning, nor crying, nor pain; they will be no longer because the first things have passed away.
And the One sitting upon the throne said, Behold, I make all things new. And He says, Write, because these are faithful and true words.
And He said to me, It is done! I am the Alpha and the Omega, the Beginning and the End. I will give to the one thirsting from out of the spring of the water of life freely (as a free gift).
The one overcoming will inherit all things and I will be his God and he will be My son:

However to the cowardly (timid, fearful of losses), and unbelieving, and having become abominable (detestable), and murderers, and fornicators (the sexually immoral), and sorcerers, and idolaters, and all liars, their portion is in the lake burning with fire and brimstone (sulphur), which is the second death.

And one from out of the seven angels came having the seven bowls being full of the seven last plagues, and spoke with me, saying, Come here, I will show you the bride (nymphen, latin-sponsam-bride), the wife (gynaika, latin-uxorem-wife) of the Lamb.
And he carried me away in the Spirit upon a great and high mountain and showed me the holy city Jerusalem, descending from out of heaven away from God,
Having the glory of God. Its radiance was similar to a most precious stone, like as a jasper stone being clear as crystal;
Having a great and high wall, having twelve gates and twelve angels at (upon, over) the gates, and names having been inscribed which are the names of the twelve tribes of the sons of Israel.

From the east (rising of the sun, latin-oriente) were three gates (latin-portae-gates like portals); and from the north, three gates; and from the south, three gates; and from the west (setting of the sun), three gates.
And the wall of the city having twelve foundations, and upon them the twelve names of the twelve apostles of the Lamb.

And the one speaking with me had a golden measuring reed, so that he should measure the city, and its gates, and its walls. stadia, about 1500 miles-NASB and AMP). The length, and the breadth, and the height of it are equal.

And the city lies foursquare (laid out as a square), and its length is the same as the width. And he measured the city with the reed at (upon) twelve thousand stadia (12,000 stadia, about 1500 miles-NASB and AMP). The length, and the breadth, and the height of it are equal.
And he measured its wall, a hundred forty four cubits (about 72 yards-NASB-AMP), the measure of man, that is, of an angel.
And the structure (material of the building, latin-structura) of the wall, of it jasper; and the city pure gold similar to clear glass.
The foundations of the wall of the city having been adorned with every precious stone; the first foundation, jasper; the second, sapphire; the third, chalcedony; the fourth, emerald;
The fifth, sardonyx; the sixth, sardius; the seventh, chrysolite; the eigth, beryl; the ninth, topaz; the tenth, chrysoprase; the eleventh, jacinth; the twelfth, amethyst.
And the twelve gates, twelve pearls; each one of the gates respectively was from out of one pearl; and the street of the city pure gold like as transparent (able to shine through) glass.
And I saw no temple in it; for the Lord God Almighty is its temple- and the Lamb.
And the city has no need of the sun nor of the moon so that they should shine in it; for the glory of God enlightened it and the Lamb is its lamp.
And the ethnicities will walk by its light; and the kings of the earth bring their [ethnicities] glory and honor (valuing) into it.
And its gates shall not be shut by day; for there will not be night.
And they will bring the glory and the honor of the ethnicities into it.
And there never shall enter into it anything defiling and those practicing abomination and a lie; only those having been written in the book of life of the Lamb.
And he showed me a river of water of life, bright (shining, splendid) as crystal, flowing forth from out of the throne of God and of the Lamb.

In the middle of its street, and on this side and on that side of the river, the tree of life, producing twelve fruits, yielding its fruit according to each month; and the leaves of the tree unto the healing (therapeian, latin-sanitatem-health) of the ethnicities.
And there will not be any longer any curse; and the throne of God and of the Lamb will be in it; and His servants (bond-slaves) will serve Him.
And they will see His face; and His name will be upon their foreheads.
And there will be no night and they have no need of the light of a lamp and the light of the sun; because the Lord God will enlighten upon them and they will reign unto the ages of the ages.
And he said to me, These words are faithful and true; and the Lord, the God of the spirits of the prophets, sent His angel to show His servants the things that must come to pass in quickness.
And Behold, I am coming quickly. Blessed is the one keeping the words of the prophecy of this book.
And I, John, am the one hearing and seeing these things. And when I heard and saw, I fell down to worship in front of the angel showing me these things.
And he says to me, See not [to do this]: I am your fellow servant and of your brothers, the prophets, and of those keeping the words of this book. Worship God!
And he says to me, Do not seal the words of the prophecy of this book; for the time is near.

The one being unrighteous (unjust), let him be unrighteous still; and he who is filthy, let him be filthy still; and he who is righteous (just), let him practice righteousness (justice) still; and he who is holy, let him be holy still.
Behold, I am coming quickly, and My reward (wages) is with Me, to give (give back, return) to each as is his work.
I am the Alpha and the Omega, the First and the Last, the Beginning and the End.
Blessed are those washing their robes so that there will be their authority (right) upon the tree of life and they shall enter into the city by the gates.
Outside are the dogs, and the sorcerers, and the fornicators (sexually immoral), and the murderers, and the idolaters, and everyone loving and practicing falsehood.
I, Jesus, have sent My angel to testify these things to you in the churches. I am the Root and the Offspring of David, the Bright (latin-splendida) Morning Star.
And the Spirit and the bride say, Come! And the one hearing, let him say, Come! and the one thirsting let him come; the one wanting, let him take the water of life freely.
I testify to everyone hearing the words of the prophecy of this book, If anyone should add upon these things, God will add upon him the plagues having been written in this book.
And if anyone should take away from the words of the book of this prophecy, God will take away his part from the tree of life and from out of the holy city, of those having been written in this book.
He testifying these things says, Yes, I am coming quickly. Amen. Come, Lord Jesus!
[The grace of the Lord Jesus Christ be with all the holy [ones]. Amen.]—this is not in Latin Vulgate but is in most English translations.
